2604.07466.title: Cross-Tokenizer LLM Distillation through a Byte-Level Interface
2604.07466.paper_summary: Cross-tokenizer distillation (CTD), the transfer of knowledge from a teacher to a student language model when the two use different tokenizers, remains a largely unsolved problem. Existing approaches rely on heuristic strategies to align mismatched vocabularies, introducing considerable complexity. In this paper, we propose a simple but effective baseline called Byte-Level Distillation (BLD) which enables CTD by operating at a common interface across tokenizers: the byte level. In more detail, we convert the teacher's output distribution to byte-level probabilities, attach a lightweight byte-level decoder head to the student, and distill through this shared byte-level interface. Despite its simplicity, BLD performs competitively with--and on several benchmarks surpasses--significantly more sophisticated CTD methods, across a range of distillation tasks with models from 1B to 8B parameters. Our results suggest that the byte level is a natural common ground for cross-tokenizer knowledge transfer, while also highlighting that consistent improvements across all tasks and benchmarks remain elusive, underscoring that CTD is still an open problem.
2604.07466.paper_summary_zh: 跨標記器蒸餾（CTD），即當教師和學生語言模型使用不同的標記器時，從教師到學生的知識轉移，仍然是一個大多數未解決的問題。現有的方法依賴於啟發式策略來對齊不匹配的詞彙，這引入了相當大的複雜性。在本文中，我們提出了一個簡單但有效的基線，稱為字節級蒸餾（BLD），它通過在標記器之間的共同介面——字節級別上運作來實現CTD。更詳細地說，我們將教師的輸出分佈轉換為字節級概率，將一個輕量級的字節級解碼器頭附加到學生上，並通過這個共享的字節級介面進行蒸餾。儘管其簡單性，BLD在多個基準測試中表現出與顯著更複雜的CTD方法相當的競爭力，並在多個基準上超越它們，涵蓋了從1B到8B參數的多種蒸餾任務。我們的結果表明，字節級別是跨標記器知識轉移的自然共同基礎，同時也強調在所有任務和基準上持續改進仍然難以實現，凸顯出CTD仍然是一個未解決的問題。
2604.07466.author: Avyav Kumar Singh et.al.
2604.07466.authors: Avyav Kumar Singh, Yen-Chen Wu, Alexandru Cioba, Alberto Bernacchia, Davide Buffelli
